audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra holiness and blood-life text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for slaughter centralization, offerings at tent entrance, goat-demon/seirim polemic, blood/life prohibition, and carcass washing.

verses.restored: Speak to Aaron and his sons and to all children of Israel, and say to them: This is the matter that YHWH commanded, saying,
Any man from house of Israel who slaughters ox or lamb or goat in camp, or who slaughters outside camp,

and to entrance of tent of meeting does not bring it, to bring near as offering to YHWH before dwelling-place of YHWH, bloodguilt shall be counted to that man; blood/life he has shed, and that man shall be cut off from midst of his people.

So that children of Israel may bring their sacrifices that they sacrifice on face of field, and bring them to YHWH, to entrance of tent of meeting, to priest, and sacrifice them as sacrifices of peace/well-being to YHWH.
The priest shall throw blood/life on altar of YHWH at entrance of tent of meeting and turn fat into smoke as soothing scent to YHWH.
They shall no longer sacrifice their sacrifices to goat-demons/wild goat powers after whom they prostitute. This shall be age-long statute to them throughout their generations.
You shall say to them: Any man from house of Israel or from sojourners who sojourn among them who brings up burnt offering or sacrifice,
and to entrance of tent of meeting does not bring it to do it to YHWH, that man shall be cut off from his peoples.
Any man from house of Israel or from sojourners who sojourn among them who eats any blood/life, I will set my face against soul/life eating blood/life and cut it off from midst of its people.
For soul/life of flesh is in blood/life, and I have given it to you upon altar to make atonement/covering over your souls/lives; for blood/life, by soul/life, makes atonement/covering.
Therefore I said to children of Israel: No soul/life from you shall eat blood/life, and sojourner sojourning among you shall not eat blood/life.
Any man from children of Israel or from sojourners who sojourn among them who hunts game, beast or bird that may be eaten, shall pour out its blood/life and cover it with dust.

For soul/life of all flesh is its blood/life; it is in its soul/life. So I said to children of Israel: Blood/life of any flesh you shall not eat, for soul/life of all flesh is its blood/life; everyone eating it shall be cut off.
Every soul/life eating carcass or torn animal, native or sojourner, shall wash garments and wash in water and be unclean until evening; then clean.
But if he does not wash and does not bathe his flesh, he shall bear his iniquity.

translation_notes: Blood is rendered blood/life to preserve Hebrew nefesh-in-blood logic.
Slaughter centralization is tied to sanctuary order and distancing Israel from field sacrifices to seirim/goat-powers.
Sojourner is included in blood/life prohibition, showing shared ritual boundary.
Hunted animal blood is poured out and covered with dust, honoring life rather than consuming it.
safety_note: Do not use Leviticus 17 to create gore fascination or food shame. The core concern is reverence for life, sanctuary-centered offering, and refusing exploitative or rival ritual powers.
